buckets.label: Stalled High-Value Loans
buckets.description: Above-median loans sitting at the same stage for 30+ days
buckets.action: Priority escalation — clear conditions or re-engage borrower

buckets.label: Low Probability, High Value
buckets.description: Top-quartile loan amounts with <25% funding probability
buckets.action: Assess viability — re-engage or reallocate resources

buckets.label: Rate Lock Expiring
buckets.description: Loans with rate locks expiring within 7 days that haven't reached CTC

buckets.label: No Lock at Late Stage
buckets.description: Loans past Approved without a rate lock — high drop-off risk
buckets.action: Contact borrower to secure rate lock

buckets.label: Lock Expired, Pre-CTC
buckets.description: Rate lock already expired and loan hasn't reached CTC
buckets.action: Re-lock or renegotiate — loan is at high risk of cancellation

benchmarks.source: ICE Mortgage Technology 2024, Ellie Mae OIR, LendingTree

closing_rates.note: Industry 'closing rate' is measured from formal application on a 90-day cycle (Ellie Mae/ICE, ~80% of US mortgage apps). FlexPoint's 37% pull-through is from pipeline entry — much earlier. These are NOT directly comparable.

scenarios.description: The Approved→CTC transition is the pipeline's longest and most variable stage (median 18d, P75 30+d). Loans stalled here have materially lower funding rates. Expediting condition clearance, appraisals, and borrower follow-up compresses this stage.
scenarios.current_state: Median 18d Approved→CTC · 259 loans stalled >15d at Approved
scenarios.target_state: Reduce median to 15d via weekly condition review cadence
scenarios.methodology: Historical funding-rate differential between <15d and ≤18d A→CTC cohorts, applied to loans currently >15d stalled at Approved stage
scenarios.confidence_note: Historical correlation is strong but assumes operational changes can replicate the speed of naturally fast-progressing loans.
caveats: Loans that naturally clear conditions in <15d may have simpler profiles — not all loans can be accelerated equally
Assumes ops capacity exists to run weekly condition review cadence across all stalled loans

scenarios.description: Unlocked loans at the Approved stage have significantly lower ML-predicted funding probabilities than locked counterparts at the same stage. Securing rate locks earlier signals borrower commitment and reduces dropout risk.
scenarios.current_state: 292 unlocked loans at Approved (avg prob 7%) vs 133 locked (avg prob 61%)
scenarios.target_state: Convert 40% of unlocked Approved loans to locked status
scenarios.methodology: Probability gap between locked and unlocked loans at Approved stage (from ML model), applied conservatively to 40% of currently unlocked Approved loans
scenarios.confidence_note: Lock status is strongly correlated with funding but the relationship is likely not fully causal — loans get locked because they're progressing well, not the other way around.
caveats: The probability gap (unlocked vs locked) likely reflects loan health rather than lock status causing funding
Telling ops to lock a struggling loan may not change its underlying viability
40% conversion rate may be optimistic for loans unlocked due to borrower uncertainty or rate shopping

scenarios.description: Loans sitting 30+ days at the same mid-pipeline stage (Underwriting through CTC) without progression are at high dropout risk. Targeted outreach — phone calls, condition reminders, processor follow-up — has historically recovered 20–35% of stalled loans.
scenarios.current_state: 178 loans stalled 30+ days at stages Underwriting–CTC · avg prob 9%
scenarios.target_state: Re-engage 30% of stalled loans to restore pipeline-average probability
scenarios.methodology: Probability gap between stalled loans and overall pipeline average, applied to 30% of stalled loan value (historical re-engagement success rate)
scenarios.confidence_note: Stale loans are often stale for cause — appraisal issues, borrower walkaway, income verification failures.
caveats: Industry re-engagement response rates are typically 5-15%, lower than the 30% assumed here
Many 30+ day stalled loans may be effectively dead — the conditions that caused the stall may not be resolvable
A more realistic estimate would discount this figure by 50-70%

scenarios.description: Retail loans currently fund at a fraction of the rate of Wholesale loans. This gap reflects differences in processor experience, borrower profile, and pipeline management rigor. Closing half the gap through dedicated Retail ops support, tighter SLAs, and earlier lock discipline would materially increase projected fundings.
scenarios.current_state: Retail avg prob 5% vs Wholesale 30% · 59 active Retail loans
scenarios.target_state: Improve Retail probability to 18% (halfway to Wholesale levels)
scenarios.methodology: 50% of the current probability gap between Retail and Wholesale channels applied to current Retail live pipeline
scenarios.confidence_note: The Retail vs Wholesale gap is partly structural (different borrower profiles, self-selection) rather than purely operational.
caveats: Retail borrowers may self-select differently than Wholesale — lower credit quality, more rate-sensitive
The gap may be irrecoverable through coaching alone if driven by borrower demographics
Historical retail pull-through (10.7%) vs current pipeline (5%) suggests some of the gap is cyclical

red_team_notes.overlap_explanation: Scenarios target overlapping loan populations (measured 52% overlap). A loan that is stalled, unlocked, and at Approved is counted in scenarios 1, 2, and 3. The adjusted total (57.5% upside) corrects for this double-counting.
red_team_notes.correlation_vs_causation: The model's top feature is lock_expiry_vs_month_end (0.678 importance). Rate lock status is a strong predictor but likely reflects loan health rather than causing funding. Counterfactual estimates for locking should be treated as upper bounds.
red_team_notes.confidence_summary: Only the Approved→CTC acceleration scenario has medium confidence. The rate lock and stale reactivation scenarios have low confidence due to correlation-vs-causation concerns and optimistic response rate assumptions.

recommendations.title: Secure rate locks on 3 unlocked late-stage loans
recommendations.description: These loans have reached Approved or later without a rate lock. Unlocked late-stage loans fund at ~8% vs ~42% for locked loans — contact borrowers immediately to secure pricing.
recommendations.confidence_caveat: Lock status correlates with funding but the relationship may not be fully causal. Prioritize loans where lock is the only missing piece, not loans with underlying issues.

recommendations.title: Rush CTC for 7 loans with locks expiring in 14 days
recommendations.description: Rate locks expire before these loans reach Clear-to-Close. Each day of delay risks lock expiry and borrower fallout. Prioritize condition clearance for this cohort.

recommendations.title: Clear final conditions on 26 near-certain CTC+ loans
recommendations.description: These 26 loans are at CTC or beyond with 80%+ ML probability — they represent $12.8M in near-guaranteed volume. Dedicated ops support ensures they fund this month, not next.

recommendations.title: Re-engage 17 stalled above-median loans
recommendations.description: These above-median loans have been at the same stage for 73+ days on average — longer than historical norms. Proactive borrower outreach and condition escalation can move them before month-end.
recommendations.confidence_caveat: Re-engagement works best on loans stalled due to process issues, not borrower disengagement. Industry response rates for stale pipeline are typically 10-15%.

recommendations.title: Archive 401 eliminated loans to free ops capacity
recommendations.description: 401 loans ($167.1M face value) have been flagged by the elimination filter as unlikely to fund. Formally closing them stops ops follow-up on dead deals and redirects focus to the live pipeline.

recommendations.title: Lift Retail pull-through: coach LOs on 5 at-risk loans
recommendations.description: Retail channel pull-through runs ~10% vs 42% Wholesale. 5 Retail loans have <40% ML probability. Targeted LO coaching and condition tracking can lift conversion on these deals.
